question: What is a system prompt?
options: The first message you send in a chat
An error message from the operating system
Hidden standing instructions the app gives the model before your message
explanation: The system prompt is the model's job description - rules and personality set by the app builder, invisibly inserted before every conversation.

question: What is few-shot prompting?
options: Asking the same question several times
Including a few examples of the desired output in your prompt
Using as few words as possible
Sending prompts to several models at once
explanation: Show, don't tell: a couple of examples of the format and style you want teaches the pattern more reliably than describing it.

question: Asking a model to reason step by step before answering tends to improve accuracy on hard problems.
explanation: This is chain of thought. Since models produce one token at a time, putting the working-out on the page gives them room to actually solve the problem - and it worked so well it was built into reasoning models.

question: What is prompt injection?
options: Compressing a prompt to fit the context window
Malicious text that tricks a model into ignoring its instructions
explanation: Models cannot fully separate instructions from content, so hostile text ('ignore your previous instructions...') can hijack them. Guardrails exist to limit this.

question: Match each prompt part to its role.

pairs.right: The model's job description, set by the app
Chat history, documents, and tool results the model can see

explanation: The model receives all three as one stretch of text - and what it sees is all it knows.

question: A sufficiently good prompt can let a model check today's weather by itself.
explanation: Prompting shapes what the model says, not what it can do. Reaching the outside world - weather, email, code - requires tools.
